name: EasyMCP — MCP Server Framework
what_it_does: EasyMCP — a developer-friendly framework for building MCP servers in TypeScript/JavaScript with minimal boilerplate — providing decorators, type inference, and a clean API surface to define MCP tools, resources, and prompts without the verbose SDK setup of the official TypeScript MCP SDK.
use_cases: Building custom MCP servers with less boilerplate from TypeScript developers
Rapid MCP server prototyping from developer teams
Teaching MCP server development with simpler API from educational contexts
Building internal tooling as MCP servers from enterprise dev teams
Wrapping existing Node.js APIs as MCP servers from backend teams
Creating MCP servers with decorator-based syntax from framework users
not_for: Teams not building custom MCP servers
Production systems where official SDK guarantees are needed
best_when: A TypeScript developer wants to build a custom MCP server quickly with minimal boilerplate — EasyMCP reduces the SDK ceremony significantly.
avoid_when: You need official SDK guarantees or Python. Use the official @modelcontextprotocol/sdk for maximum compatibility.

auth.notes: No authentication in framework — developers implement auth for their specific servers. Framework handles MCP protocol; auth is developer responsibility.

pricing.notes: Free community framework. No external service costs.

performance.rate_limits: Framework only — no built-in rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Community framework — may lag behind official SDK for new MCP features
Framework abstractions may not expose all MCP protocol capabilities
API may change between versions — pin version for production
Individual project — maintenance depends on community adoption
For building servers, not for direct end-user consumption
agent_readiness.security_notes: Framework only — security depends on developer implementation. Community project. No built-in vulnerabilities in framework itself.

security_score_components.security_notes: Framework — auth is developer's responsibility. Community project. No inherent vulnerabilities.
